key_departures.impact: Traded to Cleveland for a 2026 fifth-round pick. Was a versatile starter at right tackle and guard for the Texans. Departure creates a direct hole at OT, compounding an already poor offensive line.

Included as part of the David Montgomery trade package. A 2023 second-round pick who never found consistent footing; ranked near the bottom percentiles in pass and run block win rate. Loss is manageable given his limited production.
Signed a 3-year, $24M deal with Washington. Was a starting DT who logged nearly 50 combined tackles and 6.0 sacks over two seasons in Houston. Meaningful loss from the interior rotation.
Cut before the start of the league year, clearing $8M in cap space. Missed all of 2025 with a foot injury. Departure was a planned cap move, not a competitive loss.

Not expected to return per Aaron Wilson/KPRC2 reporting. Still slowed by the devastating 2023 knee injury; rushed for 506 yards on 122 carries in 2025. Departure is a depth loss but not surprising given Montgomery acquisition.
Not expected to return per Aaron Wilson/KPRC2 reporting. Was a depth receiver; his absence does not materially change the team's WR need picture given Nico Collins and returning young receivers.
Not expected to return per Aaron Wilson/KPRC2 reporting. Was a veteran rotational edge rusher. Given Hunter and Anderson are both locked up, Autry's departure is a depth loss only.
Signed with the Falcons on the first day of the legal tampering period. Was a depth linebacker behind Al-Shaair and To'oTo'o. An expected departure; with EJ Speed re-signed, the LB depth is now thin.
Pending free agent not reported to return. Was a rotational/depth edge rusher. With Hunter and Anderson at the top, this is a depth vacancy only.

signings.analysis: One-year, fully guaranteed $40.1M extension keeps one of the NFL's elite pass rushers in Houston through 2027. Hunter recorded 15 sacks and 2nd-team All-Pro honors in 2025. This was not addressing a need — it was retaining a cornerstone defender. EDGE remains a strength, not a need, alongside Will Anderson Jr.

3-year, $37.5M deal with $23.5M guaranteed keeps the Texans' best guard off the open market. Ingram, 27, was one of the best run-blocking guards in the NFL in 2025 by PFF grade after being traded from Minnesota. $23.5M in guarantees signals real investment. However, the center position remains completely unaddressed — Scruggs was traded and no center was added. IOL need is partially addressed (guard is solved) but center is now a critical hole, arguably escalating the overall IOL need.

One-year, up to $7M deal (guarantees not reported as significant) keeps the veteran tackle for another season. Brown, 32, started 7 games in 2025 and allowed just one sack in 479 snaps when healthy. However, he carries injury risk (ankle injury ended his 2025 playoffs) and is 32 years old. With Tytus Howard traded to Cleveland, OT remains a clear draft/free agency need. This is a bridge signing, not a solution — OT need remains essentially unchanged.

One-year, $12.6M extension keeps Schultz, who had a career-high 82 catches in 2025. The AAV is at the upper end of the mid-tier starter range for TE. The one-year structure limits the long-term clarity at TE — the team will need to revisit this position in 2027 and ideally draft a developmental TE to build behind Schultz.

2-year, $13M deal with $7.5M guaranteed re-signs the No. 3 LB who started 9 games in 2025 and logged 62 tackles. At $6.5M AAV he is above the depth tier, more of a rotational/spot-starter signing. He is not a replacement for Christian Harris at depth LB — he was already on the team. Combined with the Harris departure, LB depth remains thin. Al-Shaair extension talks ongoing. LB need is only minimally addressed.

2-year deal with $12M guaranteed retains Rankins, who started all 17 games in 2025 and recorded 35 tackles, 3 sacks, and two touchdowns. He mitigates the loss of Tim Settle Jr. at IDL. However, Rankins is 31 with a history of injuries and the total contract value has not been fully confirmed (using conservative estimate based on reported $12M guaranteed and 'well over $6M AAV'). The IDL need created by Settle's departure is partially addressed by retaining Rankins, but a younger developmental IDL piece is still needed.

3-year, $24.75M deal with $16.5M guaranteed is a genuine multi-year investment at safety. Blankenship, 27, started consistently for four seasons in Philadelphia, recording 308 career tackles and 9 interceptions. He pairs with Calen Bullock to give Houston a real starting safety duo — the team's biggest secondary weakness in 2025 was the revolving door at the S2 spot after MJ Stewart's torn quad. $16.5M guaranteed across a 3-year deal signals this is their guy at the position. S need is fully addressed.

Acquired via trade (Juice Scruggs + 2026 4th-round pick + 2027 7th-round pick). Montgomery has 2 years remaining on his existing deal ($5.49M in 2026, $7.49M in 2027, no guaranteed money remaining — though the Texans are expected to add guarantees). He rushed for 716 yards and 8 TDs in 2025 with Detroit, and has 33 rushing TDs over the past 3 seasons. He is a clear immediate starter and upgrade over the Marks/Chubb combination from 2025. RB need is fully addressed, though the team gave up a 4th-round pick and an IOL starter (Scruggs) as trade cost.

One-year, $2M deal (up to $2.5M with incentives) re-signs the waiver pickup DT who joined Houston in December 2025 after Settle and Edwards went down with injuries. Played 110 snaps with 6 run stops. Pure depth signing; does not address the IDL starter need left by Tim Settle's departure to Washington.

One-year, $2.5M deal brings back the veteran safety depth piece who started 4 games in 2025 before suffering a torn quad. Given the Blankenship signing addresses the starter S need, Stewart reverts to a depth/special teams role. Minimal cap hit and provides insurance for the position.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Tytus Howard was traded to Cleveland for a 5th-round pick, removing a key starting OT. Trent Brown's 1-year, up to $7M deal is a band-aid — he's 32 with significant injury history and no real guarantees reported. Houston now has potentially no locked-in starting-caliber OT other than Aireontae Ersery. The offensive line was already ranked 32nd in run block win rate in 2025. OT is now the team's single most critical draft/free agency need.

Ed Ingram's 3-year, $37.5M re-signing resolves the RG hole. However, Juice Scruggs was traded to Detroit and no center was signed. The Texans reportedly missed out on top center target Cade Mays (signed with Lions). Jake Andrews is the likely placeholder at C. IOL urgency has been partially resolved at guard but remains critical at center — tier stays at 2 with center as the specific acute need.

Tim Settle Jr. departed to Washington on a 3-year, $24M deal, creating a hole next to Rankins. Rankins' re-signing (2yr/$14M, $12M gtd) partially addresses this but he is 31 with injury history. Naquan Jones is only a $2M depth signing. Houston needs a quality IDL piece — either a mid-tier FA or a Day 2 draft pick. Need escalated from tier 3 to tier 2.

David Montgomery trade (2 years, $12.98M in existing contract) fills the backfield immediately. He has 33 rushing TDs over 3 seasons and is a proven starter-caliber power back. Combined with Woody Marks as the complement, RB need is fully resolved. Can still draft a late-round developmental back but no longer a premium need.

Reed Blankenship's 3-year, $24.75M deal with $16.5M guaranteed is a real multi-year investment at a position that was a revolving door in 2025. He pairs with Calen Bullock for a legitimate starting duo. MJ Stewart re-signs as depth insurance. Safety need is fully resolved — can drop to a luxury pick in the draft.

EJ Speed's re-signing (2yr/$13M) retains the No. 3 LB. However, Christian Harris departed to Atlanta, thinning the depth. Al-Shaair extension talks are ongoing but not yet confirmed. LB need remains at tier 3 — depth is short and a mid-to-late round draft addition remains advisable. No tier change warranted yet pending Al-Shaair situation.

Danielle Hunter's 1-year, fully guaranteed $40.1M extension locks up one of the NFL's elite pass rushers through 2027. Will Anderson Jr.'s extension talks are an offseason priority. With both Hunter (15 sacks in 2025) and Anderson (12 sacks) under contract, EDGE is a roster strength. Denico Autry's departure is a depth loss only. EDGE becomes a luxury/day-3 consideration, not a premium need.

Dalton Schultz's 1-year, $12.6M extension retains a starter-caliber tight end who had a career-high 82 catches in 2025. However, the one-year structure means this position is not solved long-term. The depth behind Schultz remains thin. Tier unchanged — the team should still seek a developmental TE in the mid-rounds of the draft.

key_additions.headline: Elite pass rusher (15 sacks, 2nd-team All-Pro in 2025) locked in through 2027 on historic fully-guaranteed deal
Best guard on the market re-signed to multi-year deal, anchoring the interior offensive line through 2028
Four-year Eagles starter fills the revolving door at S2, giving Houston a legitimate starting safety duo alongside Calen Bullock
33-TD power back acquired via trade addresses Houston's red-zone and short-yardage weakness immediately
Starting DT retained on multi-year deal, partially offsetting Tim Settle Jr.'s departure to Washington
Career-high 82-catch tight end locked in for 2026 on a one-year bridge deal
Veteran tackle returns on one-year bridge deal after Howard trade, but OT depth remains a concern
Third LB retained on two-year deal to maintain depth after Christian Harris departs to Atlanta
Waiver-claim DT brought back on minimum-value deal to bolster interior depth after Settle departure
Veteran safety depth piece returns as insurance and special teamer behind new starter Blankenship

key_losses.headline: Versatile starting OT traded for a 5th-round pick, creating Houston's most urgent unresolved positional need
Two-year starting DT returns to Washington, leaving a hole in Houston's interior defensive rotation
Third-round LB departs on Day 1 of legal tampering, thinning Houston's linebacker depth behind Al-Shaair
Veteran RB released after missing all of 2025 with foot injury, clearing cap space for Montgomery trade
Veteran back not expected to return, but departure is superseded by David Montgomery trade

key_losses.contract_summary: Traded to DET as part of Montgomery deal
key_losses.headline: Second-round IOL included in Montgomery trade package, further hollowing out the interior offensive line
